untranslatable_reason: Signal requires options-specific metrics (implied volatility term structure comparison, near/long-term IV spread differential, options Greeks, and time decay tracking) which are not available in the standard price/technical indicator set. The strategy fundamentally depends on: (1) measuring implied volatility across two different expirations (6-week vs 19-week), (2) comparing near-term IV > long-term IV spread by a specific margin, (3) tracking time decay (theta) on calendar spreads, and (4) managing defined-risk option positions. These require an options pricing and Greeks engine, not price-based technical indicators. The entry condition 'Near-term implied volatility > long-term implied volatility by a meaningful margin' cannot be approximated by available indicators.
translation_notes: Attempted to map 'elevated historical volatility' to hvol_20 threshold and 'HIGH_VOL regime' proxy using india_vix, but this misses the core signal logic which is inherently options-structure dependent. This signal requires a dedicated options backtester with IV term structure analysis, not a price-action rule engine. Parameters near_term_expiry_days=42 (6 weeks), long_term_expiry_days=133 (19 weeks) are derived from source text but cannot be operationalized without options data.
